% Summary
\section*{Summary}
Results-driven \textbf{AI/ML Engineer and Data Scientist} with \textbf{3+ years of experience} designing and deploying production-grade machine learning systems, RAG pipelines and data engineering solutions across enterprise domains including real estate, municipal government, and financial services. Skilled in applying statistical modeling, predictive analytics and machine learning techniques including supervised learning, clustering and deep learning to extract actionable insights from large-scale structured and unstructured datasets. Proven track record of building end-to-end AI systems from data ingestion and vector search infrastructure to LLM orchestration and cloud deployment on \textbf{AWS} and \textbf{Azure}. Experienced in full-stack AI development using \textbf{Python, LangChain, FastAPI, Pinecone and Apache Spark}, with strong foundations in MLOps, distributed data processing and scalable system design. Adept at translating complex business requirements into reliable, maintainable and high-impact AI and data science products.

% Work Experience
\section*{Work Experience}

\textbf{AI/ML Engineer} \hfill Feb 2026 -- Present \\
IpserLab LLC, Fort Worth, TX $|$ \textit{Product: Smart Pantry — AI-Powered Meal Planning Platform} \vspace{-11pt} \\
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Developing an end-to-end \textbf{Smart Pantry} application using \textbf{Python, FastAPI, and React (Vite)}, integrating an LLM-powered meal recommendation engine that suggests personalized recipes based on real-time pantry inventory, dietary preferences, and expiry-aware prioritization logic.

    \item Building an OCR-based grocery bill scanning pipeline to automatically parse and populate user inventory from receipt images, with manual entry fallback and schema validation using \textbf{Supabase PostgreSQL} for structured data storage.

    \item Designing and implementing LLM prompt pipelines to generate ranked meal suggestions and recipe steps, incorporating user dietary constraints (vegan, allergies, calorie/protein goals) and cuisine preferences as structured context inputs.

    \item Architecting automated inventory deduction workflows triggered post-cooking confirmation, with real-time low-stock alerting, expiry-aware prioritization, and auto-generated shopping lists derived from recipe ingredient gaps and historical usage patterns.

    \item Architecting full-stack system infrastructure using \textbf{Supabase Auth} for OTP and OAuth (Google/Apple) authentication, \textbf{Supabase Storage} for media assets, and \textbf{Neon/Supabase PostgreSQL} for relational data, with environment-based configuration management and \texttt{.env} templating for consistent cross-environment deployment.

    \item Conducting ML model evaluation and hyperparameter tuning to optimize prediction quality and classification performance, validating outputs against standard metrics (precision, recall, F1) and iterating on prompt and model configurations to improve recommendation relevance.

    \item Contributing to full-stack system design and API schema design (\textbf{FastAPI + Pydantic}), following Agile/Scrum practices with Git-based version control and structured code reviews across a distributed remote team.

    \item Collaborating on deployment planning across \textbf{Render/Vercel/Fly} hosting targets, supporting mobile-readiness with \textbf{React Native/Expo} for iOS and Android platforms.

\end{itemize}
\vspace{1pt}

\textbf{AI Developer} \hfill Jan 2025 -- Jun 2025 \\
EasyBee AI, Boston, USA $|$ \textit{Domains: Real Estate \& Municipal Government AI Agents} \vspace{-11pt} \\
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Designed, developed, and maintained multiple production-grade \textbf{Python} services powering Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows for enterprise knowledge access across real estate and municipal government domains, enabling intelligent document retrieval over structured and unstructured data sources deployed on \textbf{AWS (S3, Lambda, EC2)}.

    \item Engineered large-scale document ingestion and embedding pipelines using \textbf{LangChain} and \textbf{OpenAI} embedding models, transforming heterogeneous corpora — including property records, policy documents, and municipal FAQs — into high-dimensional vector representations for semantic search, with raw data and artifact storage managed via \textbf{AWS S3}.

    \item Built and managed vector search infrastructure using \textbf{Pinecone}, including index creation, namespace isolation, metadata enrichment, scalable document chunking strategies for long-form regulatory text, and bulk upserts to support low-latency semantic retrieval at enterprise scale.

    \item Developed automated RAG evaluation frameworks using \textbf{Python asyncio} batch pipelines to benchmark retrieval and generation quality across thousands of QA pairs, achieving \textbf{85\% accuracy} with \textbf{sub-second (0.52s avg)} latency, computing contextual relevancy, faithfulness, and accuracy metrics with structured reporting for continuous model quality assessment.

    \item Integrated structured datasets — CSV-based knowledge bases, FAQs, and policy documents — into retrieval pipelines, ensuring data normalization, schema validation, and robust handling of missing or malformed inputs with enriched contextual metadata (titles, identifiers, hierarchical attributes) to improve retrieval precision and answer grounding.

    \item Implemented end-to-end production observability through custom logging frameworks, standardized logger factories, and structured log outputs to support debugging, monitoring, and auditability; validated service reliability and retrieval correctness using \textbf{PyTest} automated test suites across all deployed AI agent services.

    \item Built reusable, modular service layers using \textbf{FastAPI} and \textbf{Docker}, decoupling embedding generation, retrieval logic, prompt orchestration, and response synthesis; containerized and productionized AI services with environment-based configuration management using \texttt{.env} files for secure secret handling and consistent deployment across \textbf{AWS} environments.

    \item Designed batch ingestion utilities with progress tracking and fault tolerance to ensure robust processing of large datasets during initial indexing and incremental updates, with pipeline orchestration and scheduling managed via \textbf{Apache Airflow}.

    \item Developed data preprocessing and enrichment workflows attaching contextual metadata to embedded documents, implementing scalable chunking and embedding strategies to handle large policy and regulatory documents without degrading retrieval relevance or downstream answer quality.

    \item Collaborated on the evolution of internal AI platforms by integrating evaluation feedback loops and enabling continuous refinement of knowledge-backed conversational agent systems, maintaining production codebases with version control hygiene, modular architecture, and cross-team collaboration standards.

\end{itemize}
\vspace{1pt}

\textbf{Application Development Analyst} \hfill Oct 2021 -- Jul 2023 \\
Accenture, Chennai, India $|$ \textit{Domains: Financial Services \& Enterprise Data Engineering} \vspace{-11pt} \\

    \item Developed and optimized ETL pipelines using \textbf{Python} and \textbf{Apache Spark} for enterprise financial services clients, improving data integration throughput by \textbf{30\%} and enabling reliable ingestion of large-scale structured and unstructured datasets across high-volume transactional workloads.

    \item Designed and managed database schemas using \textbf{MongoDB} for schema-less unstructured data and \textbf{PostgreSQL} for structured relational data ingestion, optimizing storage architecture, indexing strategies, and query retrieval performance to support downstream analytics, reporting pipelines, and real-time data access patterns.

    \item Applied \textbf{machine learning} clustering techniques — including KMeans and hierarchical clustering — to analyze employee learning patterns and behavioral segments across enterprise client engagements, improving engagement metrics and resource allocation efficiency by \textbf{15\%} through data-driven segmentation and personalized learning path recommendations.

    \item Built automated reporting pipelines and interactive dashboards using \textbf{Power BI} and \textbf{Tableau}, integrating Python-based analytical outputs with business-facing visualizations to deliver actionable insights on performance trends, KPIs, and operational metrics to financial services stakeholders across multiple concurrent engagements.

    \item Engineered data quality frameworks incorporating validation rules, schema enforcement, anomaly detection, and structured error handling to ensure the integrity and consistency of ingested data across upstream and downstream systems in production environments.

    \item Designed and automated end-to-end data workflows including ingestion, transformation, aggregation, and export stages, reducing manual intervention and improving pipeline reliability and repeatability across recurring batch processing jobs.

    \item Incorporated comprehensive unit testing and system-level integration testing using structured test suites, with robust logging and alerting mechanisms to proactively identify failures, monitor pipeline health, and ensure production stability across client-facing data services.

    \item Collaborated closely with business analysts and client stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical specifications, data models, and pipeline designs, ensuring alignment between engineering deliverables and enterprise reporting objectives.

    \item Managed end-to-end project delivery workflows using \textbf{JIRA} for ticket tracking, sprint planning, and progress reporting in an Agile/Scrum environment, maintaining \textbf{Git} version control hygiene and enforcing branching strategies across team repositories.

    \item Mentored junior developers through structured code reviews, pair programming sessions, and documentation of engineering best practices, improving team delivery velocity, reducing bug rates, and maintaining high-quality output standards across concurrent client engagements.

% Projects
\section*{Projects}

\href{https://github.com/Aadarsh-Praveen/VabGen-Rx}{\textbf{VabGenRx — AI-Powered Clinical Drug Safety and Decision Support Platform}} $|$
\href{https://yellow-sea-05177870f.2.azurestaticapps.net/login}{Live Demo} \hfill March 2026 \\
\vspace{-10pt}
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Architected a production-ready multi-agent clinical decision system using \textbf{Python, FastAPI,} and the Microsoft Agent Framework on \textbf{Azure AI Foundry}, coordinating five specialized agents responsible for safety analysis, disease contraindication evaluation, dosing analysis, patient counselling generation, and final orchestration to generate comprehensive medication safety reports in under \textbf{90 seconds}.

    \item Designed and implemented the full system architecture integrating a \textbf{React + Vite} frontend with Redux state management, a \textbf{Node.js} authentication server using JWT and bcrypt, and a \textbf{Python FastAPI} backend connected to \textbf{Azure SQL} databases, enabling secure clinical workflows including patient record management, laboratory analysis, prescription safety validation, drug interaction warnings, and multilingual patient counselling generation.

    \item Built an evidence retrieval pipeline integrating \textbf{PubMed NCBI E-utilities API} and \textbf{FDA OpenFDA API} with \textbf{Azure SQL} caching and concurrency controls (20 PubMed / 3 FDA concurrent requests) to prevent rate-limit violations, implementing a four-level evidence confidence framework to ensure grounded clinical outputs and prevent unsupported conclusions in generated safety recommendations.

    \item Implemented precision dosing analysis based on patient laboratory parameters including eGFR, potassium, TSH, and bilirubin against FDA label thresholds, supporting detection of renal impairment, hepatic risk, and contraindications while generating patient-specific dosing recommendations using \textbf{GPT-4o}.

    \item Developed a compounding risk detection system using \textbf{GPT-4o} to analyze drug-drug interactions, drug-disease relationships, and dosing signals simultaneously, identifying multi-factor clinical risks affecting the same physiological pathway and triggering conditional re-evaluation phases when compounding signals are detected.

    \item Deployed the complete system on \textbf{Microsoft Azure} using Azure App Service, Azure Static Web Apps, Azure SQL, \textbf{Azure Key Vault} (\texttt{DefaultAzureCredential} passwordless authentication), Azure Blob Storage, and an Azure Logic App warm-ping to eliminate cold starts; configured a \textbf{9-alert Azure Application Insights} monitoring suite with \textbf{OpenTelemetry} distributed tracing covering critical failures, HIPAA audit breaches, agent timeouts, LLM failures, orchestrator fallbacks, slow response latency, and A2A task failures.

    \item Implemented HIPAA-compliant security infrastructure including \textbf{RBAC} by medical specialty, SHA-256 hashed patient identifiers, 90-day password expiration, 15-minute session timeout, a physically isolated Azure SQL audit database, and 6-year PHI log retention enforced on service startup.

    \item Integrated multilingual patient counselling generation supporting \textbf{100+ languages} using \textbf{Azure OpenAI GPT-4o} translation with composite-key caching (\texttt{drug|sex|age\_group|habits}, 30-day TTL) to optimize repeated counselling requests across similar patient profiles.

    \item Exposed a standards-compliant Agent-to-Agent (A2A) interoperability endpoint via \textbf{Azure MCP}, enabling external agents to programmatically discover and invoke four VabGenRx skills — full safety analysis, drug interaction analysis, dosing recommendation, and patient counselling — with full task lifecycle management (\texttt{submitted → working → completed | failed}).

    \item Established a \textbf{CI/CD pipeline} using \textbf{GitHub Actions} for automated deployment and wrote a comprehensive test suite covering agent behavior, dosing logic, counselling generation, and translation accuracy; achieved a \textbf{100\% pass rate} across all evaluation categories (relevance, coherence, fluency, groundedness) on 15/15 test cases using Microsoft Foundry AI-assisted evaluation on a custom \texttt{drug\_disease\_eval.jsonl} dataset.

\end{itemize}
\vspace{5pt}

\href{https://github.com/Aadarsh-Praveen/amazon-electronics-recommendation-system}{\textbf{AI-Powered Semantic Recommendation Engine}} $|$
\href{https://amazon-electronics-recommendation-system-nyh2l8xc5kvnn2fxmyslf.streamlit.app/}{Live Demo} \hfill Dec 2025 \\
\vspace{-10pt}
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Built a production-grade AI recommendation system processing \textbf{6.7M Amazon reviews} across 31,100 products, achieving \textbf{85.4\% NDCG@10} using hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense BGE embeddings + cross-encoder reranker) with \textbf{\textless 1s latency}.

    \item Engineered a scalable ETL workflow with \textbf{Polars} lazy execution to clean, deduplicate, and filter 6.7M raw reviews to 5.9M high-quality samples, enriched with summarization, sentiment labels, and aspect extraction across 10+ product categories using a multi-model \textbf{NLP} stack including \textbf{Pegasus} (abstractive summarization), \textbf{RoBERTa} (sentiment), and \textbf{zero-shot NLI} (aspect extraction).

    \item Deployed GPU-accelerated \textbf{Pegasus} summarization on \textbf{AWS SageMaker (ml.g5.12xlarge, 4×A10G GPUs)} using intelligent chunking (300 tokens/chunk) and batching (40 products/run), generating \textbf{32K+ summaries} in 12--15 hours using 208 checkpoints for fault-tolerant processing.

    \item Designed a hybrid search algorithm optimizing fusion weight ($\alpha = 0.65$), increasing NDCG@10 from \textbf{68\% (BM25-only) → 78\% (hybrid) → 85.4\% with reranker} (+17.4\% total improvement); built a \textbf{Qdrant Cloud} vector DB backend hosting 31K product embeddings (384-dim) and a 122MB BM25 index with \textbf{4-level LRU caching} achieving \textbf{10--15× speedup} (545ms → 50ms).

    \item Created a \textbf{FastAPI} REST service (/search, /health, /stats, /cache-stats) with \textbf{Pydantic} validation, structured logging, SQLite analytics, and full CORS support; instrumented component-level latency profiling (embedding 45ms, dense 120ms, BM25 85ms, reranker 280ms) and built real-time monitoring dashboards with \textbf{Plotly}.

    \item Designed a cost-efficient hybrid cloud architecture combining \textbf{GCP VM} backend, \textbf{Streamlit Cloud} frontend, and \textbf{Qdrant Cloud} vector DB at \textbf{\$25/month} operating cost; resolved complex deployment blockers including HuggingFace rate limiting, Docker model caching (740MB), NumPy serialization issues, and Python 3.10--3.12 dependency conflicts, achieving \textbf{99.5\% production uptime}.

    \item Built an interactive \textbf{Streamlit} UI with semantic search, aspect-based filters, sentiment visualization, and reranker toggles, deployed serverlessly with zero infrastructure management overhead.

\end{itemize}
\vspace{5pt}

\href{https://github.com/narayana-3005/RAG-based-Financial-Analysis-Chatbot}{\textbf{RAG-Based Financial Analysis Chatbot}} \hfill Dec 2024 \\
\vspace{-10pt}
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Engineered a modular RAG execution pipeline supporting document loading, embedding generation, vector indexing, retrieval, and response synthesis as independent reusable components; orchestrated cloud-native storage using \textbf{Google Cloud Storage (GCS)} for raw documents, indexed data, and intermediate artifacts.

    \item Established \textbf{CI/CD pipelines} via \textbf{GitHub Actions} for automated testing, build validation, and deployment; implemented data and experiment versioning with \textbf{DVC} for reproducible model behavior and traceability across dataset and pipeline updates.

    \item Built automated monitoring utilities to log model predictions, track system metrics, and push runtime statistics; integrated \textbf{Slack webhook} alerting for pipeline failures, retraining events, and anomalous system behavior, with retraining hooks and evaluation scripts for continuous retrieval and generation improvement.

    \item Applied infrastructure-as-code and configuration-driven design using \textbf{YAML}-based workflow definitions for CI/CD, monitoring, and cloud build automation, structuring the project for extensibility toward additional data sources, alternative embedding models, and deployment targets.

\href{https://github.com/Aadarsh-Praveen/Food101}{\textbf{Food Image Classification (Food-101)}} \hfill Sep 2025 \\
\vspace{-10pt}
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Built an end-to-end image classification pipeline for large-scale visual recognition on the \textbf{Food-101} dataset using \textbf{TensorFlow} and \textbf{Keras}, leveraging transfer learning with an \textbf{ImageNet}-pretrained \textbf{EfficientNetV2B0} backbone and a custom classification head with global average pooling and softmax output across 101 food categories.

    \item Implemented a multi-stage training strategy (feature extraction → partial fine-tuning → full fine-tuning) with \textbf{mixed-precision computation (mixed\_float16)} to enable faster convergence and efficient GPU utilization without compromising numerical stability; applied Adam optimizer scheduling, learning-rate reduction on validation plateaus, and early stopping to stabilize training dynamics and mitigate overfitting.

    \item Engineered a high-throughput \textbf{tf.data} input pipeline with dataset shuffling, batching, resizing, and prefetching to maximize training efficiency on large image datasets; integrated \textbf{TensorBoard} experiment tracking with timestamped run directories and checkpointing mechanisms to persist best-performing model weights based on validation accuracy for reproducibility and downstream evaluation.

    \item Designed an inference utility to perform single-image prediction with human-readable class labels; evaluated model performance using standard classification metrics on held-out validation data, analyzing learning curves to diagnose convergence behavior and generalization quality across all 101 food categories.

\end{itemize}
\vspace{5pt}

    \item Designed modular experimentation workflows using \textbf{Scikit-learn, TensorFlow,} and \textbf{Keras} to support hyperparameter tuning, architecture exploration, and reproducible experimentation; documented dataset characteristics, modeling assumptions, experimental findings, and future optimization directions to support transparency and iterative improvement.

\href{https://github.com/Aadarsh-Praveen/CustomerSegmentationUsingRFMAnalysis}{\textbf{Customer Segmentation using RFM Analysis}} \hfill Dec 2023 \\
\vspace{-10pt}
\begin{itemize}[noitemsep]

    \item Performed end-to-end customer segmentation and business intelligence analysis using \textbf{RFM methodology} on a real-world e-commerce dataset (\textbf{500K+ transactions, 4K customers, 37+ countries}) to derive actionable marketing insights, with all data processing and aggregation performed using \textbf{Python (Pandas, NumPy)}.

    \item Implemented Recency-Frequency-Monetary (RFM) scoring to quantify customer value and applied \textbf{KMeans clustering (k=3)} after feature standardization to classify customers into New, Prospective, and Loyal segments, enabling differentiated lifecycle marketing and retention strategies.

    \item Identified peak purchasing behavior (highest order volume on Thursdays, peak hour at 12 PM) and computed an average repeat purchase cycle of \textbf{$\sim$35 hours}, revealing high short-term re-engagement potential and opportunities for automated remarketing campaigns.

    \item Quantified a \textbf{2.27\% return/refund rate} across total orders, identified SKUs with 100\% cancellation rates, and computed product-level profit and profit margin metrics to rank top 5 margin-generating products — supporting inventory rationalization, quality control decisions, and margin-optimization strategy.

    \item Conducted geographical analysis using choropleth visualization identifying the \textbf{UK} as the highest order volume region and \textbf{Netherlands} as the highest average order value region; performed time-series transaction analysis across 2010--2011 detecting Q4 seasonal spikes to enable demand forecasting and inventory planning insights.

    \item Developed interactive visualizations using \textbf{Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly} to communicate revenue trends, geographic distribution, profit margins, and customer behavior patterns; delivered business recommendations including targeted promotions for high-value clusters, SKU rationalization for high-cancellation products, and region-specific pricing optimization strategies.

\end{itemize}
